key_points: Secularism means that the state treats all religions equally
India is a secular country where no religion is considered the official religion
The Constitution guarantees the freedom to practice and follow any religion
The government does not favour or discriminate against any religion
Secularism helps maintain peace and harmony among people of different religions
The state can intervene in religious matters if practices violate equality or justice
examples: People in India can celebrate different religious festivals freely
Citizens have the right to follow any religion such as Hinduism, Islam, Christianity or Sikhism
The government can stop discriminatory religious practices
Courts can protect citizens if their religious rights are violated
formulas: Secularism → Equal respect for all religions
Secular state → No official religion + Equal treatment
Religious freedom → Right to practice + Right to choose religion
exam_tips: Write the definition of secularism clearly
Mention that India has no official religion
Explain the role of the Constitution in protecting religious freedom
Give simple examples of religious equality in India
